questions.question_text:
Look at the decision screen below:
{{ decision_screen_belief1_prob }}

The screen shows a Bayesian updating task. Bag A contains 75% red balls and 25% blue balls. Bag B contains 25% red balls and 75% blue balls.

There was a 30% chance Bag A was selected and a 70% chance Bag B was selected. 1 red ball(s) were drawn.

What do you think is the probability (in %) that Bag A was selected? Enter a number between 0 and 100.

Look at the decision screen below:
{{ decision_screen_belief1_cu }}

You just estimated the probability that Bag A was selected in the previous task.

How certain are you that the optimal guess is within 1 percentage point of the number you just stated?

Answer on a scale from 0% (not at all certain) to 100% (completely certain), in steps of 5%.
